summary_text: I inspected the tree and lockfiles. - Package manager baseline - All three directories use npm lockfiles (`package-lock.json`, lockfileVersion `3`). - Files checked: - [/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/package.json](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/package.json) - [/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/local-agent/package.json](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/local-agent/package.json) - [/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/cockpit-pwa/package.json](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/cockpit-pwa/package.json) - No `workspaces` field in any `package.json`. - No `pnpm-workspace.yaml` / workspace config found. - Workspace structure and propagation - This is **not** a workspace/monorepo install model; it is three sibling dependency graphs: - root - `local-agent/` - `cockpit-pwa/` - `npm install` at the root does **not** propagate dependency installation into `local-agent` or `cockpit-pwa`. - Per-folder install is the normal repair path. - Arm64-native optional dependency hotspots (from lockfiles) - Root: - `@cloudflare/workerd-darwin-arm64` / `@cloudflare/workerd-linux-arm64` (via `workerd` optional deps through `wrangler`) - `@esbuild/*-arm64` binaries via `esbuild` - `fsevents`/`sharp` are present as optional transitive deps - `local-agent`: - `better-sqlite3` (explicit dependency, native module; has install script + `prebuild-install`) - `@esbuild/*-arm64`, `@rollup/*-arm64` (optional) - `fsevents` appears as optional transitive - `cockpit-pwa`: - `sharp` via optional dependency graph (Next ecosystem path), plus many `@img/sharp-*` arm64 variants - `@esbuild/*-arm64`, `@rollup/*-arm64` optionals Minimal repair sequence (arm64 safest route) 1. Cleanly reinstall each graph in place: - `cd /Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy && npm ci` - `cd local-agent && npm ci` - `cd ../cockpit-pwa && npm ci` 2. If you only need to repair native optional packages without full re-resolve: - Run `npm rebuild <module>` in the same three directories for the failing modules (common: `better-sqlite3`, `sharp`, `@cloudflare/workerd-*`, `@esbuild/*-arm64`). 3. Re-run with `--include=optional` if your environment previously used optional-skipping flags. Risk/notes - High-risk area is `local-agent` because `better-sqlite3` is a real native dependency. - Cockpit/Root sharp/workerd/esbuild arm64 bits are optional; if they fail, rebuild/install from source may be needed on that machine. - Because this is not a workspace, there is a real chance of partial/accidental success only from parent `node_modules` resolution; keep installs per-folder for deterministic, reproducible state.
